However, since the Al-Y-Y hollow already converged there, directly targeting Y-Y bridge might give the same result. Instead, let me try a different untested site that could potentially be lower energy.

The Al-Y bridge has not been directly tested. Given that Y-rich environments seem favorable (Y-Y bridge gave best energy), an Al-Y bridge might also be interesting. However, the Au-Y bridge already slipped to Au-Y-Y hollow.

Actually, looking more carefully: the available hollow-3 sites are Al-Al-Y, Al-Au-Y, Al-Y-Y, Au-Y-Y. We've effectively sampled Al-Y-Y (slipped to Y-Y bridge), Al-Au-Y (reached by slip), Au-Y-Y (reached by slip twice). Al-Al-Y has not been sampled at all. Let me try Al-Al-Y hollow directly.

plan.reasoning:
SMILES Consistency Check: The SMILES [NH2] represents an NH2 radical. The RDKit analysis confirms: index 0 is N, SP3 hybridized, degree 0 (no heavy atom neighbors), radical_electrons = 1, formal_charge = 0. This is indeed a reactive radical species (aminyl radical). The binding atom is N at index 0 with 1 unpaired electron, confirming ReactiveSpecies type.

History Analysis: No previous attempts. This is the first plan.

Adsorbate Analysis: [NH2] is an aminyl radical with N as the sole heavy atom (index 0) carrying 1 radical electron. It will bind through N to the surface.

Surface Analysis: The surface is a ternary alloy Al-Au-Y. Available sites include ontop (Al, Au, Y), bridge (Al-Al, Al-Au, Al-Y, Au-Y, Y-Y), and hollow (Al-Al-Y, Al-Au-Y, Al-Y-Y, Au-Y-Y, Al-Au-Au-Y).

Strategy: For NH2 radical adsorption, the N atom typically prefers high-coordination sites on metal surfaces. Y is an early transition/rare-earth metal that tends to form strong bonds with N. I will start with a hollow site involving Y atoms, specifically Al-Y-Y hollow (3-fold), as Y-rich environments tend to bind N strongly. The N atom (index 0) will bind at this hollow site.

Plan: ReactiveSpecies, hollow site, Al-Y-Y, adsorbate_binding_indices = [0].

plan.reasoning:
SMILES Consistency Check: [NH2] has one N atom (index 0) with SP3 hybridization and 1 radical electron. This is a reactive NH2 radical, so adsorbate_type = 'ReactiveSpecies'. The binding atom is N (index 0).

New Plan: Since the hollow Al-Y-Y slipped to Y-Y bridge, I should now explicitly test other distinct sites to see if a lower energy exists. Ontop Y is a clean, untested site that could yield a different (possibly lower) energy configuration. If Y-ontop also slips to Y-Y bridge, that would confirm convergence and I should terminate. Alternatively, I could try Au-Y bridge or Al-Y bridge. Given that Y appears to be the dominant binding element (the slip went to Y-Y), I'll try Y ontop first as it is a high-symmetry, chemically distinct site.
